delete.summary: Hard delete the current user account

post.summary: Admin login
post.description: Admin-first MFA rollout: if the account has not enabled authenticator-app MFA, tokens are returned immediately. If MFA is enabled, the response contains mfaRequired=true plus a challengeToken, and the frontend must call POST /auth/admin/mfa/verify-login.

201.description: Returns either login tokens or an MFA challenge.

401.description: Email was not found or password is incorrect.

403.description: Account is not allowed to access the admin panel.

post.summary: Send a Firebase password reset email for an admin account

403.description: Only admins can reset password here or account is disabled

post.summary: Complete admin password reset with Firebase oobCode

400.description: Reset code is required or invalid/expired

post.summary: Login with Google ID token
Login with Apple idToken or authorization code
Apple web callback (form_post)

requestBody.description: Apple callback payload (form_post). Provide either code or id_token.

post.summary: Login/register via social id
Send a Firebase password reset email for a mobile user account
Complete mobile password reset with Firebase oobCode

400.description: Reset code is required or invalid/expired

get.description: Returns current authenticator-app status for the logged-in admin. MFA is off by default until the admin enables it.

supportedMethods.reason: Requires a transactional email OTP provider; Firebase Auth email OTP requires Firebase Auth sign-in/MFA flows.
Reserved for a future SMS provider integration.

403.description: Only admin and super admin accounts can manage MFA.

post.summary: Start admin authenticator-app setup
post.description: Creates a pending TOTP enrollment and returns both the raw secret and otpauthUrl. Frontend should render the otpauthUrl as a QR code.

401.description: Current password was wrong or JWT is invalid.

403.description: Only admin and super admin accounts can manage MFA.

post.summary: Verify admin authenticator-app setup
post.description: Confirms the first six-digit code from the authenticator app and enables MFA for the admin. Recovery codes are returned only once.

401.description: The submitted authenticator-app code was invalid.

403.description: Only admin and super admin accounts can manage MFA.

post.summary: Disable admin MFA
post.description: Disables MFA for the current admin. Requires the current password plus either a fresh authenticator-app code or a recovery code.

401.description: Current password was wrong or the provided TOTP/recovery code was invalid.

403.description: Only admin and super admin accounts can manage MFA.

post.summary: Complete admin login after MFA challenge
post.description: Submit the challengeToken returned by the admin login endpoint plus either a six-digit authenticator-app code or a recovery code.

201.description: MFA challenge verified and tokens issued.

401.description: Challenge token was invalid/expired or the factor code failed.

get.description: Returns gathering/event records with speakers and editable child tracks. Pagination is currently returned as a single page to match existing admin content endpoints.

post.summary: Create a gathering
post.description: Creates the parent gathering/event. Send speaker and track arrays as JSON strings in multipart form-data. Speaker photos can be uploaded in `speakerPhotos`; use `speakerPhotoIndexes` when photos are sparse or not in speaker order.

requestBody.description: Create a gathering/event with an optional cover photo, up to 50 keynote speakers, and editable child tracks. If `tracks` is omitted, a default `Keynotes` track is created.

thumbnail.description: Alias for coverPhoto. Also accepts image or file for compatibility.

speakers.description: JSON array, max 50. Each item supports name and optional sortOrder.

speakerPhotos.description: Speaker image files. By default file order maps to speakers array order.

speakerPhotos[].description: Alternate field name accepted by the API for speakerPhotos.

speakerPhotoIndexes.description: Optional JSON indexes mapping uploaded speakerPhotos to speakers.

tracks.description: JSON array of child categories/tracks. If omitted, a default Keynotes track is created.

get.summary: Get one gathering with speakers, tracks, and videos

patch.description: Updates parent gathering fields and optionally replaces speakers or tracks. When replacing speakers/tracks, include existing item ids to preserve them; omitted existing items are removed.

requestBody.description: Update a gathering/event. Passing `speakers` or `tracks` replaces that collection; include existing item ids to keep/edit them.

thumbnail.description: Alias for coverPhoto. Also accepts image or file for compatibility.

speakers.description: JSON array, max 50. Include speaker `id` to update an existing speaker.

speakerPhotos.description: Speaker image files. By default file order maps to speakers array order.

speakerPhotos[].description: Alternate field name accepted by the API for speakerPhotos.

tracks.description: JSON array. Include track `id` to rename/reorder an existing child category.

post.summary: Create a gathering child category/track
post.description: Creates an editable child category under a gathering, such as Keynotes, Track 1, Track 2, or a custom admin-provided track name.

patch.summary: Rename or reorder a gathering track

post.summary: Create direct video upload sessions for a gathering track
post.description: Preferred flow for large videos. Creates Vimeo TUS upload sessions and stores the videos under the selected gathering track/category.

requestBody.description: Create direct Vimeo TUS upload sessions for videos under a gathering child category/track.

metadata.description: JSON array. Required fields per item: sourceFileName and sourceFileSize. Optional: title, description, duration, sourceMimeType, attachmentTitle.

thumbnails.description: Optional video thumbnail files. Order maps to metadata order unless thumbnailIndexes is supplied.

attachments.description: Optional attachment files, such as slides or notes. Order maps to metadata order unless attachmentIndexes is supplied.

get.description:
Use this route for article list screens and carousel sections in the mobile app.
Frontend can build a carousel by requesting a small `limit` with optional `topic`, `category`, or `tag` filters.
Examples:
- Latest articles: `GET /mobile/articles?page=1&limit=10`
- Featured carousel: `GET /mobile/articles?topic=featured&limit=10`
- Category rail: `GET /mobile/articles?category=discipleship&limit=10`
- Inline search: `GET /mobile/articles?search=disciple maker&page=1&limit=20`
For a dedicated search UX route, frontend may also call `GET /mobile/articles/search`.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Optional search term forwarded to Renew.org WordPress post search.

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: Optional Renew topic slug filter. Use this for official WP topic groupings such as featured articles.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of articles to return.

200.description: Normalized paginated articles list for mobile.

source.description: Indicates whether the response came from a live WordPress fetch, fresh cache, stale cache, or an empty fallback response.

400.description: Validation failed for the list query params.

get.description:
Dedicated mobile articles search route.
This returns the same normalized paginated shape as `GET /mobile/articles`, but the `search` query is required.
Frontend can use this route for explicit search screens while keeping article list rendering identical.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional category slug to narrow the search.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Optional tag slug to narrow the search.

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: Optional topic slug to narrow the search.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of articles to return.

source.description: Indicates whether the response came from a live WordPress fetch, fresh cache, stale cache, or an empty fallback response.

400.description: Validation failed for the search query params.

get.description:
Use this route when a user taps an article card from any list or carousel.
The response includes normalized HTML content, author/byline information, taxonomy terms, published date, and the canonical Renew.org share URL.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Article slug returned by the articles list or search route.

source.description: Indicates whether the response came from a live WordPress fetch, fresh cache, stale cache, or an empty fallback response.

404.description: No published article exists for the given slug.

503.description: Renew.org WordPress articles upstream is unavailable and no cached detail response exists.

get.description:
Use this first when the user opens the Events tab in mobile.
The response contains the featured event card plus the collection tiles/cards for the first screen.
Workflow:
1. Call this route to render the events landing page.
2. Render `data.collections` as the collection/category cards.
3. When a user taps a collection, call `GET /mobile/events/collections/:collection` with the selected collection slug.
4. When a user taps an event card, call `GET /mobile/events/:slug` using the slug returned from the list.
5. If `registration.mode` is `external`, open `registration.url` in a browser or webview.

200.description: Landing-page payload for the first Events UI screen.

source.description: Indicates whether the response came from a live WordPress fetch, fresh cache, or stale cache.

warning.example: Live Renew.org events were unavailable. Serving recently cached event data.
warning.description: Present when the API had to serve stale cached event data because the upstream source was unavailable.

get.summary: Collection page for an events category
get.description:
Use this after the user taps a collection tile on the Events landing page.
The response includes both the selected collection metadata for the hero/header area and the paginated event cards for that collection screen.
Collection behavior:
- `regional` is curated from the Regional Events page and hydrated against canonical TEC event records.
- `online` is curated from the Online Events page and hydrated from canonical Renew.org `live-event` URLs.
- `past` is sourced from the Past Events archive page and augmented with canonical TEC past-event records when they exist.
Frontend note: use `data.collection` for the top banner/title and `data.events.items` for the list cards.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: Collection slug from the Events landing page. Recommended values for the current UI are `regional`, `online`, and `past`.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Optional search term scoped to the selected collection screen.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: 1-based page number for the selected collection.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return for the selected page.

200.description: Collection-specific page payload for the second Events UI screen.

source.description: Indicates whether the response came from a live WordPress fetch, fresh cache, or stale cache.

warning.example: Live Renew.org events were unavailable. Serving recently cached event data.
warning.description: Present when the API had to serve stale cached event data because the upstream source was unavailable.

400.description: Validation failed for the collection slug or query params.

get.description:
Use this for direct browse, search, or admin-driven frontend exploration flows.
For the landing-page workflow, prefer `GET /mobile/events/home` followed by `GET /mobile/events/collections/:collection`.
This route still supports direct `type`, `category`, `search`, and optional `collection` filtering when needed.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Primary feed type for The Events Calendar-backed results. Defaults to `upcoming`.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Raw WordPress category slug for direct feed filtering. Do not use for app-level collections like regional or online.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: Optional app-level collection override. Preferred for the main mobile UI: use `/collections/:collection` instead.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return.

source.description: Indicates whether the response came from a live WordPress fetch, fresh cache, or stale cache.

warning.example: Live Renew.org events were unavailable. Serving recently cached event data.
warning.description: Present when the API had to serve stale cached event data because the upstream source was unavailable.

400.description: Validation failed for one or more query params.

get.description:
Use this after the user taps an event card from either the generic list or a collection page.
The slug comes from `items[].slug` in the list response.
This route first checks the primary TEC event detail endpoint and then falls back to curated live-event or past-archive resolution when needed.
The detail payload is normalized for the full mobile event screen and can include structured speakers, informational sections, schedule blocks, venue map data, a detail CTA block, and a supporting flyer image when Renew.org exposes them.
Registration guidance: if `registration.mode` is `external`, send the user to `registration.url` because registration still lives on the Renew.org side.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Event slug returned by an events list item.

200.description: Detailed event payload for the event detail screen.

source.description: Indicates whether the response came from a live WordPress fetch, fresh cache, or stale cache.

warning.example: Live Renew.org events were unavailable. Serving recently cached event data.
warning.description: Present when the API had to serve stale cached event data because the upstream source was unavailable.

404.description: No event matched the supplied slug in either the primary feed or curated collections.

503.description: WordPress detail lookup failed and no cached fallback was available.

get.description:
Use this first for the mobile ebooks home screen.
The response includes one featured ebook, the available topic filter chips, and the first page of latest ebooks already imported into the app backend.
This route is backed only by the local ebooks table and private file-import pipeline, not by live Renew.org reading URLs.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of latest ebook cards to return in the home payload.

200.description: Landing-page payload for the first mobile ebooks screen.

source.description: Indicates whether the response came directly from the ebooks database, fresh cache, stale cache, or an empty fallback response.

get.description: Returns the topic filter chips derived from imported ebooks already stored in the backend database.

source.description: Indicates whether the response came directly from the ebooks database, fresh cache, stale cache, or an empty fallback response.

get.description:
Use this for the generic ebooks browse screen and topic-filtered lists.
Results come only from ebooks already imported into the app backend and stored privately, so the mobile app is not dependent on live Renew.org availability.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of ebooks to return.

info.title: Renew University API
info.description: API documentation for Renew University backend

idToken.description: Google ID token from Google Sign-In SDK
Apple identity token from Sign In with Apple

authorizationCode.description: Apple authorization code (use this when idToken is not provided)

socialId.description: Provider user id (e.g. Google sub / Apple sub)

currentPassword.description: Current account password. Required before creating a new authenticator-app enrollment.

label.description: Friendly label shown back to the UI for this MFA method. Defaults to "Authenticator app".

factorId.description: Factor id returned by the TOTP setup endpoint. This identifies the pending enrollment.

code.description: Six-digit code from the authenticator app after scanning the setup QR.

currentPassword.description: Current account password. Required before disabling authenticator-app MFA.

factorId.description: Optional enabled factor id to disable. Omit to disable all enabled MFA factors for the account.

code.description: Six-digit code from the authenticator app. Required if recoveryCode is not provided.

recoveryCode.description: Single-use recovery code returned when MFA was enabled. Required if code is not provided.

challengeToken.description: Challenge token returned by the login endpoint when MFA is required.

code.description: Six-digit code from the authenticator app. Required if recoveryCode is not provided.

recoveryCode.description: Single-use recovery code returned when MFA was enabled. Required if code is not provided.

dryRun.description: When true, discover WooCommerce ebook imports without writing to DB or Firebase

limit.description: Limit how many WooCommerce ebook products are processed

concurrency.description: Bounded concurrent imports for controlled backfill

force.description: When true, re-import PDFs even if the ebook is already imported

dryRun.description: When true, discover ebook cover optimization candidates without writing to DB or Firebase

limit.description: Limit how many ebooks are processed in this maintenance run

concurrency.description: Bounded concurrency for controlled ebook cover optimization imports

force.description: When true, re-import cover images even if a stored cover asset already exists

dryRun.description: When true, discover Firebase-backed image optimization candidates without writing to DB

limit.description: Limit how many Firebase-backed media images are processed in this maintenance run

concurrency.description: Bounded concurrency for controlled signed URL refresh and variant generation

targets.description: Optional subset of Firebase-backed media types to refresh. Defaults to all supported targets.
